children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall establish and maintain a database with an inventory of the Nation’s levees.December 16, 2016

children.label: A
children.text: location information of all Federal levees in the Nation (including global information system information) and updated levee information provided by States, regional districts, Indian tribes, Federal agencies, and other entities;

children.label: B
children.text: utilizing such information as is available, the general condition of each levee; and

children.label: C
children.text: an estimate of the number of structures and population at risk and protected by each levee that would be adversely impacted if the levee fails or water levels exceed the height of the levee.

children.heading: Availability to Federal, State, regional, tribal, and local governmental agencies
children.label: A Availability to Federal, State, regional, tribal, and local governmental agencies
children.text: The Secretary shall make all of the information in the database available to appropriate Federal, State, regional, tribal, and local governmental agencies.

children.heading: Availability to the public
children.label: B Availability to the public
children.text: The Secretary shall make the information in the database described in paragraph (2)(A), and such other information in the database as the Secretary determines appropriate, available to the public.

children.heading: Inventory and inspection of levees
children.label: b Inventory and inspection of levees

children.heading: Federal levees
children.label: 1 Federal levees
children.text: The Secretary, at Federal expense, shall establish an inventory and conduct an inspection of all federally owned and operated levees.

children.heading: Federally constructed, nonfederally operated and maintained levees
children.label: 2 Federally constructed, nonfederally operated and maintained levees
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an inventory and conduct an inspection of all federally constructed, non-federally operated and maintained levees, at the original cost share for the project.

children.heading: Participating levees
children.label: 3 Participating levees
children.text: For non-Federal levees the owners of which are participating in the emergency response to natural disasters program established under , the Secretary shall establish an inventory and conduct an inspection of each such levee if the owner of the levee requests such inspection. The Federal share of the cost of an inspection under this paragraph shall be 65 percent.section 701n of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a one-time inventory and review of all levees identified in the national levee database.

children.heading: No Federal interest
children.label: 2 No Federal interest
children.text: The inventory and inspection under paragraph (1) does not create a Federal interest in the construction, operation, or maintenance of any levee that is included in the inventory or inspected under this subsection.

children.heading: Review criteria
children.label: 3 Review criteria
children.text: In carrying out the inventory and review, the Secretary shall use the levee safety action classification criteria to determine whether a levee should be classified in the inventory as requiring a more comprehensive inspection.

children.label: A
children.text: allow an official of the State, regional district, or Indian tribe to participate in the review of the levee; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide information to the State, regional district, or Indian tribe relating to the location, construction, operation, or maintenance of the levee.

children.heading: Exceptions
children.label: 5 Exceptions
children.text: In carrying out the inventory and review under this subsection, the Secretary shall not be required to review any levee that has been inspected by a State, regional district, or Indian tribe using the same methodology described in paragraph (3) during the 1-year period immediately preceding , if the Governor of the State or chief executive of the regional district or tribal government, as applicable, requests an exemption from the review.June 10, 2014

children.label: A
children.text: identify the specific engineering and maintenance deficiencies, if any; and

children.label: B
children.text: describe the recommended remedies to correct each deficiency identified under subparagraph (A), and, if requested by owner of a non-Federal levee, the associated costs of those remedies.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: In identifying deficiencies and describing remedies for a levee under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall consult with relevant non-Federal interests, including by providing an opportunity for comment by those non-Federal interests.
